You want design consistency across your Claude Code apps?

Add: logo and design elements SVG, brand colors and other relevant information. Result is image attached. This is the building block for every web app.

There is so much discord and debate about titles in design: UX Designer, Software Designer, Product Designer, and now Product Experience. At the core, I’ve always considered myself an Interaction Designer, which is enduring to any technology ever introduced.

Whether it was pixels or context windows, it’s always about interaction.

Catherine Shannon
1d

Apple’s so-called “liquid glass” has introduced more friction to the UX than possibly any update prior. It’s not intuitive, the contrast is too low, it feels nostalgic, and it’s glitchy and slow. Simultaneously too much happening and not enough if that’s even possible. Y2Kish look is kind of cute in certain contexts but it doesn’t feel “liquid,” it feels like that thin plastic shell they use in packaging that cuts your hand
